author: Jakob Unterwurzacher
message:
Introduce "-j" flag and enable multithreading per default

As before, on a 4-core Intel(R) Core(TM) i5-3470 CPU @ 3.20GHz:

$ make speed
CGO_ENABLED=0 go build "-ldflags=-X main.GitVersion=v2.2.1-19-gafb9886"
hyperfine "./cshatag -q -recursive /tmp/linux-3.0"
Benchmark 1: ./cshatag -q -recursive /tmp/linux-3.0
  Time (mean ± σ):      1.234 s ±  0.022 s    [User: 3.048 s, System: 0.603 s]
  Range (min … max):    1.201 s …  1.272 s    10 runs

author: Jakob Unterwurzacher
message:
Add "make speed"

$ make speed
CGO_ENABLED=0 go build "-ldflags=-X main.GitVersion=v2.2.1-17-gf79e480-dirty"
hyperfine "./cshatag -q -recursive /tmp/linux-3.0"
Benchmark 1: ./cshatag -q -recursive /tmp/linux-3.0
  Time (mean ± σ):      3.265 s ±  0.013 s    [User: 2.912 s, System: 0.504 s]
  Range (min … max):    3.245 s …  3.293 s    10 runs

author: Jakob Unterwurzacher
message:
Switch to charlievieth/fastwalk, which is supposedly even faster

And more importantly, supports concurrency. Concurrency will
be enabled later.

Performance now.

$ hyperfine "./cshatag -q -recursive /tmp/linux-3.0"
Benchmark 1: ./cshatag -q -recursive /tmp/linux-3.0
  Time (mean ± σ):      3.250 s ±  0.005 s    [User: 2.916 s, System: 0.487 s]
  Range (min … max):    3.244 s …  3.258 s    10 runs

author: Jakob Unterwurzacher
message:
Switch to filepath.WalkDir, which is supposedly faster

Before:

0 jakob@brikett:~/code/cshatag$ hyperfine "./cshatag -q -recursive /tmp/linux-3.0"
Benchmark 1: ./cshatag -q -recursive /tmp/linux-3.0
  Time (mean ± σ):      3.389 s ±  0.016 s    [User: 2.922 s, System: 0.548 s]
  Range (min … max):    3.375 s …  3.424 s    10 runs

After:

0 jakob@brikett:~/code/cshatag$ hyperfine "./cshatag -q -recursive /tmp/linux-3.0"
Benchmark 1: ./cshatag -q -recursive /tmp/linux-3.0
  Time (mean ± σ):      3.385 s ±  0.149 s    [User: 2.933 s, System: 0.500 s]
  Range (min … max):    3.266 s …  3.669 s    10 runs

author: Jakob Unterwurzacher
message: Add -cpuprofile flag

author: Jakob Unterwurzacher
message:
Rewrite decision logic using a truth table & fix #41

Things got too tangled to fix https://github.com/rfjakob/cshatag/issues/41 .
The new truth table is in decision.go.

Fixes https://github.com/rfjakob/cshatag/issues/41

author: Jakob Unterwurzacher
message: Warn if user.xattr.sha256 is too short / incomplete

author: Jakob Unterwurzacher
message:
README: man 2.12.1 formatting changes

Looks like man-db 2.12.1 fixed a bug where the actual
generated width was less than what MANWIDTH said:
https://gitlab.com/man-db/man-db/-/commit/4b7b65edb24fc2d40363cb1139d28320a647f4b0

	man-db$ git describe --contains 4b7b65edb24fc2d40363cb1139d28320a647f4b0
	2.12.1-pre1~18^2~1

My Fedora box now has man 2.13.0, so "make" reformatted
the man page.

author: Jakob Unterwurzacher
message:
Represent missing values as nil

Instead of using a special value (like unixtime 0) to represent
missing values, use nil pointers.

Fixes https://github.com/rfjakob/cshatag/issues/39

author: Jakob Unterwurzacher
message:
tests: add 1970 test

     `../cshatag foo.txt &> 12.out' failed with status 5

https://github.com/rfjakob/cshatag/issues/39
